canonical_definition: The latent ability of a model to perform well on tasks where the required information is contained within a short input context.
papers: Long Context Compression with Activation Beacon
LongPO: Long Context Self-Evolution of Large Language Models through Short-to-Long Preference Optimization

definitions.text: The latent ability of a model to perform well on tasks where the required information is contained within a short input context.
definitions.paper: LongPO: Long Context Self-Evolution of Large Language Models through Short-to-Long Preference Optimization

definitions.text: The set of core abilities demonstrated on tasks with short input contexts, such as general knowledge and reasoning, which should be preserved after modification.
definitions.paper: Long Context Compression with Activation Beacon

relationships.paper: Make Your LLM Fully Utilize the Context
relationships.evidence: For short-context evaluation, we followed Llama2 to test on MMLU (Hendrycks et al., 2020), Math (Hendrycks et al., 2021), and BigBenchHard(BBH) (Suzgun et al., 2022). (Section 4.1)

Long Context Compression with Activation Beacon

relationships.paper: Make Your LLM Fully Utilize the Context
relationships.evidence: In Table 3, we compare Activation Beacon with the original LLM (Full) on popular benchmarks, including MMLU (Hendrycks et al., 2021), ARC-Challenge (Bhakthavatsalam et al., 2021), BoolQ (Clark et al., 2019), and GSM8K (Cobbe et al., 2021). We can observe that Activation Beacon leads to very little performance degradation on short-context tasks. In other words, the short-context capabilities are well preserved.
